title: Delaney Stymiest-Losier
content:
## Delaney Stymiest-Losier
People.ContactEmail dstymiestlosier@mccarthy.ca
- University of New Brunswick
Delaney Stymiest-Losier is an associate in our Business Law Group in Toronto. Her practice focuses on International Trade and Regulatory Law.
Before joining the Firm as an associate, Delaney worked at Global Affairs Canada. While there, she provided trade policy guidance and issue resolution arising from Trade Commissioner services on diplomatic missions abroad.
Delaney completed her articling with the Office of the Law Clerk and Parliamentary Counsel of the House of Commons. During law school, Delaney was the Chairperson of the Law Students’ Society, Coordinator for the UNB Imprint Trans ID Clinic, President of the UNB International Law Society, Associate editor for the Law Journal, and worked as a research assistant while completing a directed research paper on International Investment Law. She also received several awards, including the Zoë Odei Memorial Scholarship, the Brian Rees Campbell Scholarship, and the City of Fredericton Public Innovation Award, for Property Taxation proposal. She holds a Bachelor of Arts Degree in International Relations from Mount Allison University and is fluently bilingual in French and English.
Delaney was called to the Ontario Bar in 2023 and is a member of the Law Society of Ontario.
